title: State of the Planet
description: News from the Columbia Climate School

items.title: In an Alpine Plant Species, Ancient Alleles May Help Drive Climate Change Adaptation
items.description: Researchers found that two alleles in the wood pink plant species may allow the perennials to adapt to warming temperatures.

items.title: Sinking Land Drives Hidden Flood Risk in One of the World’s Most Populated Regions
items.description: A new study finds that land subsidence is outpacing ocean-driven sea-level rise along the northern coastline of Java Island, Indonesia.

items.title: Ian Hunt Wrote the Climate Book He Wanted To Read as a Kid
items.description: An M.A. in Climate and Society alum shares the inspiration behind his new children’s book about climate action.

items.title: Two Sustainability Students See Opportunity Hidden in Laundry-Induced Microplastic Pollution
items.description: Students and faculty at Columbia’s M.S. in Sustainability Management and Sustainability Science programs turned cutting-edge research on microplastics into Moby Filter, a sustainability startup tackling laundry-induced pollution at its source.

items.title: Argentina Reforms Glacier Protection Law
items.description: A historic glacier protection law is under attack in Argentina as Congress loosens environmental protections.

items.title: A Complicated Future for a Methane-Cleansing Molecule
items.description: A new model shows how levels of the “atmosphere’s detergent” may rise and fall in response to climate change.

items.title: Make Every Voice Count This Earth Day
items.description: We all have the power to protect our planet, this month and beyond.

items.title: From Classrooms to Climate Impact: Two Careers Flourish in Singapore
items.description: M.A. in Climate and Society alums Amanda Chen and Anuka Upadhye tackle climate change from halfway around the world.

items.title: As Climate Change Exacerbates Extreme Weather, Olive Oil Feels the Squeeze
items.description: Intensifying droughts and extreme heatwaves are having a profound impact on olive quality, quantity and price, according to recent research.

items.title: Centering Community in Climate Resilience and Disaster Preparedness
items.description: M.S. in Climate student Allison Karabu reflects on the importance of community-based initiatives and finding the right questions to ask to effect real change.
